fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with a substantial contract win, but trades at a high valuation and carries significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 242.4%; next earnings date was 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 242.4% — strong earnings performance.
$2.6 billion agreement with Nebius — significant new business.
Stock has outperformed industry over the past 6 months.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a high mean target.
negatives: Forward P/E of 70.61 and Price/Sales of 35.45 indicate a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 is extremely high.
Profit margin of 0.2% and Return on equity of 1.3% are very low.
Beta of 3.83 suggests high volatility relative to the market.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) has seen mixed signals recently, with one report highlighting it as a top future stock while another noted it was in the red alongside FuelCell Energy amidst sector weakness. Despite this, BE's stock has outperformed its industry over the past six months, and it recently announced a significant $2.6 billion agreement with Nebius. The company also reported a strong earnings surprise in its last quarter, beating estimates by over 200%, though its next earnings date has already passed.
